Use Case Description: User creates a new UniMove account via the Customer Mobile App with email, password, full name, and phone number. The system stores credentials in PostgreSQL and assigns the customer role.
Registered customer signs in with email and password. The system validates credentials and returns a JWT access token for authenticated API requests.
User requests a password reset via email. The system sends an OTP; the user enters the OTP and a new password to complete the reset.
User signs up or signs in using Google OAuth. The system creates or links a user profile from the Google identity.
Customer views and updates profile information such as full name, phone, student ID, university, and avatar. Email and role cannot be changed through the profile API.

First-time users complete onboarding screens explaining UniMove services. Returning users view the home dashboard with shortcuts for booking, combo packages, labor service, Pass đồ marketplace, and recent activity.
Customer browses verified transport providers with filters for price, rating, and distance, then sorts results before selecting a partner.
Customer enters or selects pickup and delivery locations on the map. The system may suggest recent places from order history and validate route distance.

Use Case: Select move schedule and dorm details
Use Case Description: Customer selects the scheduled pickup date and time and enters dorm or move details such as items, floors, and elevator availability.
Customer chooses a transport package, including combo packages (Economy, Standard, Premium) or standard partner-based service options.
Customer describes items to be moved (furniture, appliances, boxes) to help estimate price and required vehicle size.
Customer selects a cargo insurance plan (none, basic, standard, or premium) before checkout on a move booking.
Customer books standalone labor (loading/unloading) or adds labor to an existing move order by configuring helpers, hours, and floors.
System calculates and displays a price breakdown including base fare, distance, floor fees, labor, insurance, discounts, and deposit amount.
Customer confirms booking details and creates a transport order linked to the selected provider, package, addresses, and pricing. Initial order status is pending.
For quotation-based bookings, customer reviews provider quotes and accepts one offer before proceeding to payment.
Customer views a detailed provider profile including service packages, rating summary, and recent reviews before selection.

Use Case: View recent places and reference prices
Use Case Description: Customer reuses recent pickup or delivery addresses from order history and views reference pricing guides before booking.
Customer enters a promotion code at payment. The system validates the code and applies the discount to the order total.
Customer pays the deposit (typically 30%) via PayOS QR code or payment link. PayOS webhook confirms payment and the order proceeds to provider matching.
Customer monitors the order lifecycle including pending, accepted, picking up, in progress, completed, and cancelled states.
During an active order, the customer views the provider location on Google Maps. The provider app sends GPS updates and the system refreshes ETA.
Customer and provider exchange real-time messages on an active transport order. Message history is stored in the database.
Customer cancels an order according to platform policy. The system shows refund estimate and updates order and payment status.
After order completion, the customer submits a star rating and review comment for the assigned provider.
Customer views past and active orders, filters by status, and opens order detail or tracking screens.
Customer views and manages saved payment methods, wallet balance, and payment history in the Payments section.
Customer receives in-app and push notifications for order updates, chat messages, Pass đồ deals, and promotions.
Buyer browses peer-to-peer used-item listings on the student marketplace with filters by province, category, and keyword.
Seller posts a listing with title, description, images, category, condition, pickup area, and price (free or negotiable).
Seller pays the listing fee via PayOS when required before the listing becomes publicly visible.
Buyer expresses interest in a listing and chats with the seller to negotiate price. Seller confirms the deal with one buyer at the agreed price.

Use Case: Book transport for Pass đồ item
Use Case Description: After deal confirmation, buyer books UniMove transport with pickup fixed to the listing area, delivery to the buyer address, and standard or combo booking flow.
Buyer confirms item receipt after delivery and rates the seller. The listing is marked as completed.
Seller updates listing status (active, hidden, reserved, closed), bumps listing visibility, and views interested buyers.
Transport business registers via the Provider Mobile App with business information and documents. Account is created with provider role and pending verification status.
Provider signs in with email and password or Google. The system issues a JWT for provider-scoped API access.
Provider uploads license, ID card, vehicle registration, and insurance documents for admin review.
Provider tracks verification state as pending, approved, or rejected, including admin notes.
Provider views today's orders, earnings summary, and key operational metrics on the dashboard.
Provider reviews new order requests and accepts or declines them with an optional reason. Customer is notified of the decision.
Provider views customer contact, pickup and delivery addresses, items, pricing, and chat for a specific order.
Provider starts the move, sends real-time GPS location, uploads delivery proof photo, and marks the order as completed.
Provider exchanges messages with the customer on an active order or related marketplace context.
Provider views earnings history, available balance, and configures bank account details for payout.
Provider creates and edits service packages including vehicle size, base price, per-km rate, helpers, and insurance options.
Provider views and updates availability schedule to control when new orders can be accepted.

Use Case: Submit quote and respond to reviews
Use Case Description: Provider submits price quotes for quotation-based orders and views or responds to customer reviews.
Administrator authenticates to the Admin Web Dashboard with admin role credentials.
Administrator views KPIs such as GMV, revenue, order count, active users and providers, commission, and summary charts.
Administrator searches, views, and updates customer accounts including suspend or activate actions.
Administrator lists providers, reviews uploaded documents, and approves or rejects provider verification with notes.
Administrator views all platform orders, filters by status, inspects order detail, and force-cancels orders when required.
Administrator reviews dispute evidence and chat logs, decides resolution, and approves full or partial refunds processed via PayOS.
Administrator moderates customer and marketplace reviews by hiding, unhiding, or flagging policy-violating content.
Administrator creates and publishes system announcements or targeted notifications to users.
Administrator views revenue analytics, provider performance, order statistics, and audit logs of admin actions.
Administrator reviews and approves or rejects provider withdrawal requests for earned revenue.
Visitor views the public marketing website with service introduction, pricing information, FAQ, and contact support.

Use Case: Register or log in from website
Use Case Description: Visitor creates an account or signs in from the public website entry point and is redirected to the app authentication flow.
Node.js API validates Bearer JWT on protected routes and enforces role-based access for customer, provider, and admin.
System creates PayOS payments, handles webhooks idempotently, and updates payment and order status including deposits and refunds.
System stores and distributes provider location updates and chat messages between participants in near real-time.
System sends FCM push notifications triggered by order, chat, payment, and marketplace events.

Use Case: Provide map services and file storage
Use Case Description: System provides address autocomplete, geocoding, and routing via Google Maps API, and uploads images to cloud storage for avatars, listings, and documents.
